current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: Execute the first proxy run for the gated architecture family at K=128 to establish whether this new family deserves incubation.
Crash-recovery proxy run: use TopK at the K=128 anchor with a modest LR reduction to re-establish a stable quality baseline.
Crash-recovery proxy run: use TopK at the K=128 anchor with a modest LR reduction to re-establish a stable quality baseline. full

recent_round_summaries.hypothesis: A gated SAE with a separate support-selection encoder can reduce support interference versus plain TopK at K=128, improving FVU before spending effort on smaller-K or cost optimizations.
Run the newly added gated SAE prototype at the existing K=128 anchor to get the first quality signal on whether separate support gating can materially improve FVU over plain TopK.
Return to the known-working TopK baseline at K=128 and slightly reduce learning rate to improve run stability after consecutive crashes without changing the architecture search surface.

gated.design_hypothesis: Run the newly added gated SAE prototype at the existing K=128 anchor to get the first quality signal on whether separate support gating can materially improve FVU over plain TopK.
next_steps: If gated improves proxy FVU, do a gated-family stabilization pass on gate sharpness or magnitude defaults.
If gated is quality-neutral but slower, separate implementation cost from architectural value before discarding it.
If gated clearly regresses FVU, pivot to a different architecture family rather than spending many rounds on local tuning.

next_move_guidance: If this TopK recovery run is stable, compare whether Adam further improves stability or FVU at the same K=128 anchor.
If stability is restored but FVU remains weak, resume architecture exploration from a different family rather than over-tuning TopK.
If this still crashes, investigate execution-layer/runtime causes before drawing conclusions about SAE quality.
